Adventure #6—The Write Stuff
Adventure #7—Oh, What a Tangled People helped themselves to pastries from the new open display. The selling floor was packed with customers	Web We Weave Previous Lesson Next Lesson	Mark as Complete
creeping—books in hand—continuously around our new circular format, picking up more books as they went.Adventure #8—To Sleep, Perchance
to Come Up with Great Ideas
Adventure #9—The Beginning Others came in the door and were handed a stack of books from a salesperson—most of which I felt confident
enough by now, having observed the process many times, that they would purchase. I watched others, who	Postscript... Four Years Later
would normally have left empty-handed or with just a book or two.
Instead, they were eagerly leaving with a stack of books they had not yet paid for—90 percent of which, in seven
days, would automatically turn into sales, as we processed their card.
Without saying anything, I passed Tom my phone to show him the text I’d received from my landlord earlier that
day:
Thanks so much for paying off the balance of your rent. Appreciate your including interest and pre-paying
next month’s rent as well.
I notice your store is packed most of the time these days, and I keep hearing good things about everything
that’s going on. Whatever you’ve been doing lately—keep it up!
Then, I asked Tom the question that had been bothering me for some time. “Tom, since I own a bookstore, I’ve
had a chance to read dozens of books on creativity and problem solving. Yet, until you came along with your
boxes, nothing ever really worked for me. Why do you think that is?”
“Two reasons,” Tom replied. “The first is application . I made sure that you applied what you learned as soon as
you learned it by asking you questions and making you come up with ideas.
“Applying what you learn,” he continued, “not only puts what you learn to work immediately—always a good
thing—it also keeps you from forgetting it by securing it to a web of actual applications. So, always apply what
you want to retain immediately —in any way you possibly can.
“The second reason these techniques worked for you was motivation . You were about to lose your bookstore
and you were very motivated to prevent that from happening. That’s tremendously energizing. It keeps you from
procrastinating. It forces you to apply what you know. And it helps you make better decisions, because there are
very unpleasant consequences to a bad decision.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 4 --

“Makes sense,” I concurred, recalling the incredibly difficult conversation I’d had to have with my daughter the
night I met Tom. Because of the work Tom and I had done together, I’d had the pleasure of having a very different
conversation with her recently, that had filled me with joy.
“That’s why, when I’m stuck on a problem, I often pretend my life depends on solving it successfully. I ask myself,
‘What would I do if my life depended on solving this problem?’ And lo and behold, I find myself suddenly
becoming much more resourceful and much more innovative.”
“Hopefully,” I said, “from now on my dire straits will just be pretend. And now that I have all those boxes to look in
for answers, I’m sure they will be.”
“I’m sure they will be, too,” agreed Tom, reaching over and helping himself to a pastry.
Our newest employee approached with a worried look on her face. “Sir,” she began, “a customer wants a book
by the end of the week and it’s just impossible—”
“Hold on,” I interrupted, holding my hand out while casting Tom a wink and a smile. “You just started with us, so
you probably don’t know. But that word is forbidden here. Now, let’s talk about what is possible and see if we
can’t help this customer…”
Tom just smiled as he bit into his pastry.
Summary & Exercises
In a Nutshell
This Adventure is about making the ideas and techniques of Idea Power a permanent part of your personal and
professional life.
Unfortunately, much information and advice we receive remains intellectual stimulation that never affects what we
do. Or we just forget it altogether.
Don’t let that happen to what you’ve learned here! These ideas and techniques are much too valuable for that
and have far too much potential to change your life for the better. But only if you put them to use.
Here’s what you can do instead…
First: find your motivation. Understand why it’s important to have better ideas and what that could do for you
in your business and your personal life. Feel how the lack of good ideas may be holding you back—limiting your
income, as well as your ability to form better relationships and achieve what you were meant to and capable of
achieving.
Then, make these techniques second nature by applying them every chance you get. Download and make
copies this Pocket Idea Generator. Keep a copy at home, at the office, in your pocket or on your phone.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 4 --

Click here to download this PDF
Use the boxes whenever you need to come up with an idea or a solution. Each time you do, not only do you
increase the odds of coming up with a much better idea or solution, but the boxes also become a part of your
thinking.
Eventually, they simply become second nature––and you won’t even need to refer to them.
Your Action Steps
1. Find your motivation. What will having better ideas do for you? Why do you want to have more and better
ideas? How will having more and better ideas improve your business and professional life?
2. Practice using these boxes and techniques every chance you get . Look for opportunities to use them.
Because the more you use them, the better you’ll become at using them––and the more you’ll get out of them
when you do!
Well, this is the end of our journey together—for now.
MODULES
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 4 --

But it’s only the beginning of your journey if you continue to use these techniques. Nothing can have a bigger
impact on your life, career or business than better ideas and better solutions to the problems you face every day.
One more thing: I want to hear how you’re doing! You’ll find a sheet that will help you track your success at the
back of your Mindbook and if you click on the Let Us Know About Your Idea Power Successes button in the
Resources section of each Adventure.
I’d love it if you’d send me a copy after you’ve filled it out with a story of how a better idea helped you resolve a
tricky problem.
Just drop me a line at David.Deutsch@MarketingRebel.com .
Apply what you learn immediately.
Raise the stakes in order to motivate and focus yourself.
All of these resources are also available in your Idea Power Mindbook if you prefer to write
them out by hand.
Let Us Know About Your Idea Power Successes
